title: Syria: Unprecedented investigation reveals US-led Coalition killed more than 1,600 civilians in Raqqa 'death trap'
strengths: Most explicit retained coalition-attributed civilian death claim for the full Raqqa assault.
limitations: Produced after the battle and focused heavily on coalition-caused harm; it does not provide a full all-actor citywide fatality accounting.
notes: Retained wording: more than 1,600 civilian lives lost as a direct result of coalition air and artillery strikes; coalition admitting to about 10% of killings.

title: Rhetoric versus Reality / Raqqa investigation summary
pages_or_sections: Summary of coalition-admitted deaths versus external estimate
strengths: Useful paired view of external coalition-caused estimate and coalition-admitted minimum.
limitations: Focused on coalition-caused deaths rather than full all-actor civilian losses.
notes: Retained wording: at least 1,600 civilians died in coalition strikes; coalition had admitted 159 deaths by 24 April 2019.

title: All Feasible Precautions? Civilian Casualties in Anti-ISIS Coalition Airstrikes in Syria
pages_or_sections: Raqqa-related case material on coalition strikes where ISIS fighters were present
strengths: Provides qualitative support that coalition attacks around Raqqa caused civilian deaths in locations where ISIS fighters were present.
limitations: Retained text is qualitative here and is not used for numeric episode estimates.
